Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement

§ 01Parties

This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement is entered into among the following parties in connection with the evaluation of a potential ESOP transaction for {{companyName}}:

{{sellerName}}
{{brokerName}}
Forhemit Stewardship Management Co.
{{advisors}}
§ 02Purpose

The parties wish to explore and evaluate a potential ESOP transaction involving {{companyName}}. In connection with this evaluation, each party may disclose Confidential Information to the other parties. This Agreement establishes the obligations of each party with respect to such information.

§ 03Confidential Information

“Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed by any party in connection with the potential transaction, including but not limited to:

A.Financial data, projections, tax returns, and quality-of-earnings analysis
B.Employee information, compensation data, and organizational structure
C.Trade secrets, customer lists, vendor relationships, and proprietary processes
D.Deal terms, valuations, capital structure, and negotiation positions
E.The identity of the seller and the existence of the potential transaction
F.Any materials marked or identified as confidential, whether written, electronic, or oral
§ 04Mutual Obligations

Each party agrees to:

A.Hold in confidence. Maintain all Confidential Information in strict confidence and protect it with at least the same degree of care used for its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care.
B.Limit use. Use Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating and facilitating the potential transaction described herein.
C.Limit disclosure. Not disclose Confidential Information to any person who is not a party to this Agreement, except as permitted under Section 05.
D.No copies. Not reproduce or copy Confidential Information except as reasonably necessary for the permitted purpose.
§ 05Permitted Disclosures

A party may disclose Confidential Information to:

A.Professional advisors. Its own attorneys, accountants, and financial advisors who have a professional duty of confidentiality and who need to know the information in connection with the transaction.
B.Legal requirement. As required by law, regulation, or court order, provided the disclosing party gives prompt notice to the other parties (to the extent legally permitted) and cooperates in seeking a protective order.
§ 06Exclusions

Confidential Information does not include information that:

A.Is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the receiving party
B.Was already known to the receiving party prior to disclosure, as documented by written records
C.Is independently developed by the receiving party without use of the Confidential Information
D.Is received from a third party who is not bound by a confidentiality obligation with respect to such information
§ 07Term

This Agreement is effective as of the date first written above and shall remain in effect for a period of twenty-four (24) months, or until the transaction closes or is formally terminated, whichever occurs first. The obligations of confidentiality survive termination for a period of two (2) years.

§ 08Return & Destruction

Upon written request by a disclosing party, or upon termination of the transaction, each receiving party shall promptly return or destroy all Confidential Information in its possession, including all copies, notes, and summaries. Upon request, a receiving party shall certify in writing that it has complied with this obligation.

§ 09No Obligation

Nothing in this Agreement obligates any party to proceed with the transaction, to enter into any further agreement, or to disclose any particular information. Each party reserves the right to withdraw from discussions at any time.

§ 10Governing Law
{{governingState}}

Any disputes arising under this Agreement shall be resolved in the courts of {{governingState}}.

§ 11Contact

For questions about this agreement, please contact:

deals@forhemit.com
{{ref}}
—Signatures

By signing below, each party agrees to the terms of this Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement.
